mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It simplifies the management of MongoDB clusters, but it is deprecated and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator repository. Key trade-offs include the need for migration to a new repository and limited support until November 2025.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for MongoDB.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MongoDB Community Operator using Helm in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a new namespace for MongoDB resources.
> Installs the MongoDB Community Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the Operator installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the MongoDB Community Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists all pods in the mongodb namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB resource to deploy a MongoDB cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 4.4.6
  storage:
    type: Durable
    size: 5Gi
EOF

```

> Creates a MongoDB ReplicaSet with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 4.4.6
  storage:
    type: Durable
    size: 5Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step checks if the MongoDB resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Lists all MongoDB resources in the mongodb namespace to verify the deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB data and resources in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Deletes the mongodb namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your MongoDB data. This step upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Backs up the current MongoDB resource configuration.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded operator is running.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed or updated.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator/master/deploy/crds/mongodb.com_mongodb_crd.yaml' to install the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MongoDB resource is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB pods may not be starting due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your MongoDB resource definition and adjust them as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm install failed: release already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The release name is already in use.

**Fix:**
Use 'helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb' to remove the existing release before reinstalling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The API version specified in the resource definition may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the API version in your MongoDB resource definition matches the installed operator version.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB 4.4.6, improved error handling during deployment.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11, ensure to check the new API versions in your resource definitions.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB resource listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb'.

  version: 4.4.6
  storage:
    type: Durable
    size: 5Gi
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
